reforms.description: In 2024, Parliament adopted legislation to reform the disability system, shifting from a purely medical to a comprehensive disability assessment, merging various payments into a single benefit, and introducing a partial disability benefit to encourage work.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to improve workforce participation among disabled individuals, thereby enhancing human capital and productivity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: Parliament adopted legislation to reform the disability system, effective September 2025.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the disability support system, aiming to enhance labor market integration and reduce dependency on benefits, which is crucial for long-term economic sustainability.
reforms.source_quote: In 2024, Parliament adopted legislation to reform the disability system, effective September 2025.

reforms.description: The government established the Directorate for Education and School Services to enhance oversight and support for schools, focusing on monitoring student achievement and providing data-driven interventions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving data collection and monitoring, the reform aims to enhance educational outcomes, which can lead to higher productivity and innovation in the long run.
reforms.status_evidence: The Directorate was operational since spring 2024.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the governance of education, aiming to improve accountability and support for schools, which is crucial for enhancing educational quality and outcomes.
reforms.source_quote: A recent governance reform is enhancing oversight of compulsory education. In spring 2024, the Ministry of Education and Children established the Directorate for Education and School Services.

reforms.description: The Tourism Policy and Action Plan for 2030 was approved by Parliament in June 2024, which includes 43 actions aimed at reducing regulatory burdens and strengthening competition in the tourism sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing regulatory burdens, the plan aims to enhance competition and foster a more dynamic tourism sector, which can lead to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan was approved by Parliament and includes specific actions in progress.
reforms.importance_rationale: The comprehensive nature of the plan and its focus on reducing regulatory burdens represent a significant structural change in the tourism sector, which is vital for Iceland's economy.
reforms.source_quote: The Tourism Policy and Action Plan for 2030 was approved by Parliament in June 2024. It includes 43 actions, some of which aim to reduce regulatory burdens and strengthen competition.

reforms.description: The new housing policy (2024-38) was approved by Parliament in September 2023, outlining comprehensive goals to address housing issues and provide accessible housing for all.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By addressing housing issues, the policy aims to improve living conditions and potentially enhance labor mobility, which can contribute to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The new housing policy was approved by Parliament.
reforms.importance_rationale: The policy represents a significant commitment to addressing housing challenges, which is crucial for social stability and economic development.
reforms.source_quote: The new housing policy (2024-38) was approved by Parliament in September 2023. It outlines comprehensive goals to address housing issues, to provide accessible housing for all, while addressing rising housing costs.

reforms.description: Iceland is reforming its product market regulations to reduce administrative burdens for startups, aiming to streamline the process of setting up limited liability companies and improve overall business dynamism.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing entry barriers and simplifying administrative processes, the reforms are expected to enhance entrepreneurship and innovation, leading to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans to reform product market regulations to boost business dynamism.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the regulatory framework affecting startups, which is crucial for fostering innovation and economic growth in Iceland.
reforms.source_quote: Iceland is reforming its product market regulations to reduce administrative burdens for startups.

reforms.description: Icelandic authorities are preparing new legislation on FDI screening to establish streamlined and systematic proceedings on national interest grounds, replacing the existing Act No 34/1991. This aims to clarify the criteria for foreign direct investment screening and provide necessary legal tools to address potential threats to national security.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By clarifying FDI screening processes, the reform aims to enhance investor confidence and attract foreign investment, which can stimulate economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities are preparing new legislation to be submitted to Parliament in autumn 2025.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the existing FDI screening framework, aiming to align with international best practices and enhance the investment climate in Iceland.
reforms.source_quote: The Icelandic authorities are preparing new legislation on FDI screening to be submitted to Parliament in autumn 2025.
